title: Increment a count on a dashborad based on status changes
excerpt:
We have a board where we list all our quotes sent to clients. There is a status column with various values eg ‘Sent’ ‘Chased’ ‘Accepted’ ‘Declined’ etc. 
If accepted, an Automation moves it to a new board (e.g. jobs in p…

posts.author: Sam1972 (Sam Townsend)
posts.cookedHtml:
We have a board where we list all our quotes sent to clients. There is a status column with various values eg ‘Sent’ ‘Chased’ ‘Accepted’ ‘Declined’ etc.

If accepted, an Automation moves it to a new board (e.g. jobs in progress).
If declined, it moves down the board to a ‘declined’ group and if its never actually accepted/declined then it gets moved to down to a group ‘archived’ after a certain lenght of time.

I’d like to be able to have a count, probably on a dashboard, that has counts of the number of ‘Accepted, Declined and Archived’ quotes. Ideally incremented when the status is changed to Accepted/Declined etc.

I’ve had a dig around but can’t find any discussion.

Many Thanks in advance.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
That should be pretty simple. Create a new dashboard, it will have you select the ‘board(s)’ you want to connect. Then add a chart, your X axis will be status and Y will be count

image1810×882 41.6 KB

image1810×882 37.5 KB

posts.author: Sam1972 (Sam Townsend)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi, thanks for the response.

It was the fact that the ‘accepted’ quotes move to a new board (and progress through a series of boards) thus its hard to total the ‘accepted’ jobs.
Your right that the declined ones that move down to a new group are easy to count on a chart. I was hoping there was some way of just incremeting a count e.g. When status changes from ‘Quote Sent’ to ‘Quote accepted’ add 1 to ‘Accepted count’ . Similarly for Declined and Expired quotes.

We have a potential work around of duplicating the ‘accepted’ row, and moving the original over to the ‘work in progress’ board as per normal, and the copy down to a separte new group. So the quotes board will have 3 groups, The Quotes Sent, Quotes Declined Archive and Quotes Accepted Archive, which can all be counted on a Dashboard chart.

posts.author: CapnHamhands (Greg Rosenberg)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @Sam1972,

There should be an automation for exactly what you asking (see image)

image1129×221 7.58 KB

Another workaround that I think would work would be to connect both the original board and the new board that the accepted quotes move to, to the dashboard and then just select which groups you want the chart to include.

image1640×1181 53 KB

In this example, the duplicate board has the two accepted status and the non duplicate has only declined.

Hope that helps,

posts.author: Valeriia.H (Valeriia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Sam1972!
Sounds like you’ve already built a really organized workflow — love that!

For what you’re looking to do (count the number of Accepted, Declined, and Archived quotes based on status changes), I highly recommend checking out Time in Status.

image1920×960 117 KB

Use Case: How to track time in Status by Person with Time in Status?

Here’s how it can help:

  • Tracks how many times each status (Accepted, Declined, Archived) is selected
  • Counts status changes — so you can see exactly how many quotes were accepted, declined, or archived
  • Works with filters — by person, by date range, by board
  • Dashboard-ready — you can add the reports to your dashboards easily
  • Keeps historical data — even after automations move items to another board!

It’s super handy if you want automatic counting without building complicated workarounds.

Here’s also a quick guide to help you set it up: Time in Status Integration Guide.

Happy to share some setup tips if you need help getting started!
